title: MD
biography: Ferid Murad was an American physician-pharmacologist of Albanian-Turkish descent who shared the Nobel Prize in 1998 for discovering nitric oxide as a signaling molecule in the cardiovascular system. His work led directly to the development of drugs like sildenafil (Viagra) and understanding of vasodilation.
aiSummary: Ferid Murad was a pharmacology specialist with an H-index of 110. Nobel Prize 1998 for discovering nitric oxide signaling in the cardiovascular system. Based in Houston, United States.

affiliations.hospitalName: University of Texas Health Science Center
affiliations.role: Director of Institute of Molecular Medicine

citations.title: Nitric oxide: physiological mediator or pharmacological product?

awards: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine (1998)

timeline.title: Discovered nitric oxide activates guanylate cyclase
timeline.description: Showed that nitroglycerin and related compounds work by releasing nitric oxide

timeline.title: Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine
timeline.description: Shared with Robert Furchgott and Louis Ignarro for nitric oxide as a cardiovascular signaling molecule
